rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JERROLD HARATANI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/22/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No supplemental inventory of the additional property identified on Schedule A-2 is on file. 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The summary of account shows gains of $24,705.62, but Schedule B shows no such gain. B) Schedule A-2 shows $24,705.62 as assets added to the conservatorship, but the summary of account does not show any additional property. C) Schedule A identifies Wells Fargo IRA Acct #x6366, but no IRA account with this account number is listed as an asset. D) Payments for meals and entertainment such as Starbucks gift cards, piano lessons, and Giant tickets appear to have been made from both the trust and conservatorship. How does the petitioner determine whether a particular expense should be charged to the conservatorship in lieu of the trust? E) What was the purpose of "Legal Fees" of $937.97 paid to the petitioner's attorneys on 12/01/2017? F) Did the court's 07/23/2018 order approve the reimbursement of costs of $665.22 to the petitioner's attorneys? G) "Costs" included in fees on account paid on 12/18/2017 and 03/08/2018 must be itemized. H) $5,000 fees on account paid on 05/17/2019 do not show any period for which the petitioner rendered services. I) How did the petitioner determine that the market value of the annuity as of 12/31/2019 was $133,169.18? J) The purchases of 445.047 shares of Fidelity Growth Strategies Fund must be itemized. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARIA MIYAMOTO AND CECILIA HARATANI LIVING TRUST DATED JUNE 6, 1994
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/22/2020. A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) Receipts and disbursements must be allocated between principal and income. If allocations are made in reliance on particular provisions of the governing trust instrument, a copy the provisions must be provided. 2) It appears that Cecilia Haratani amended the trust with respect to her share of the trust property to remove the two charities as residual beneficiaries. What part of the reported trust assets represent Cecilia's share? 3) Are TD Ameritrade's 0.30% annual custodian fees reasonable? 4) Paragraph 13 of the report only identifies the income beneficiary. Should the 3 charities designated in the trust instrument be deemed beneficiaries within the meaning of CRC 7.902 and Probate Code § 15804? 5) What were the purposes of "6 months Mgmt Fees" paid on 12/07/2018 and 08/19/2019? 6) Did the court's 07/23/2018 order approve the reimbursement of costs of $658.94 to the petitioner's attorneys? 7) What was the purpose of "Legal Fees" of $1,039.04 paid to the petitioner's attorneys on 11/05/2019? Was the payment made pursuant to prior court approval? 8) Why did Sparer Law Group, the former attorneys of record for the petitioner in both capacities as trustee and conservator, allocate 100% of their fees and costs to the trust and 0% to the conservatorship? Please note that appearances of both the petitioner and Sparer Law Group's attorney will be required at the hearing on the issue of fees and costs paid without prior court approval. *The petitioner and her attorneys need to comply with CRC 1.201 to protect personal privacy. Only the last 4 digits of financial account numbers may be disclosed in pleadings including account statements e-filed in this proceeding (viewable to the public in PES & PTR cases unless sealed). *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT M. TEETS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner avers that a bank account that was held in the name of Decedent as joint owner that received rental income was never controlled by Administrator and thus it was not a part of the estate and was not subject to reporting in his accounting. 2) Cash was not invested in an interest bearing account. 3) Personal properties located in a storage locker were not appraised or listed in an Inventory and Appraisal. 4) Petitioner requests extraordinary fees of $67,415. 5) Petitioner requests payment of paralegal fees in the amount of $7,297.50 at a rate of $350/hr. 6) Objections have been filed and responses made.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JOHN BERTRAND & VIRGINIA DAVES HOOK REVOCABLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/22/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The proof of service does not show that notice was given to the Fan Museum, a beneficiary of the fan collection under the trust amendment. Is the fan collection part of the reported tangible property of the Survivor's Trust? 2) The petitioner's attorney must file a declaration certifying the retention of original account statements. LRSF 14.93D. 3) Why is the petitioner holding title to the Sterling Bank accounts in his individual capacity? 4) How did the petitioner determine that the current bond ($189,000) was sufficient? What part of the assets of both sub-trusts represent the assets transferred from the conservatorship including their growth? 5) Is each and every security purchased during the account period an authorized investment under Probate Code § 2574(a)? 6) Why has the Bypass Trust stopped paying interest on the note to the Survivor's Trust since February 2019? 7) Were the bond premiums paid from either sub-trust during the account period? 8) Are the beginning market values of the two municipal bonds held in the Survivor's Trust (CA ST VAR PURP G/O UNTITLED and CA ST UNREFND BAL PURP G/O UNTLD) accurate? Why are they different from the ending market values stated in the first account? *The petitioner and her attorneys need to comply with CRC 1.201 to protect personal privacy. Only the last 4 digits of financial account numbers may be disclosed in pleadings including account statements filed in this proceeding (viewable to the public in PES & PTR cases unless sealed). *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing with Judge Wiss's consent, although Counsel did not provide any legal (UK) authority for the Court to find that a (lost and incompletely dated) codicil could revoke a will. Status of closing the estates is set for 1/10/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL WALTON GONZALEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For (1) Breach Of Contract; (2) Unjust Enrichment; (3) Claim To Property Under Probate Code Section 850; (4) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; (5) Breach Of Duty As Set Forth In Family Code Section 1101; (6) Violation Of Family Code Sectio 1102
rulings.Rulings: continued to 7/22/20. no later than noon on 7/15/20, Mr. Doyle must provide a file stamped copy of the amended response to Room 202.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILMA COREA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/21/20 to be heard with competing petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FIDEL ANGEL ANDRADE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (Telephonic appearnce encouraged.) Court to take the matter off calendar for failure to provide courtesy copies and a proposed order and file proof of publication. The petition also appears to be rife with errors.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER SOO HOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for petitioner to address the request to serve in pro per with no bond.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN BARRY BALDWIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. appearance required. remote appearances are encouraged. please see general court order for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF MILTON LEE HUCKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/5/20 for petitioner to file something re her efforts to verify a marriage license issued. deadline for filing is noon on 7/29/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOLORES PERKINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/29/2020. Petitioner must file a duties and liabilities form (DE-147) that includes the proposed personal representatives phone number and signed by the proposed personal representative not a supplement signed by the attorney.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN CHINN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 7/22/20. as noted in the prior tentative rulings, an amended duties and liabilities form and an amended declaration of real property must be filed. neither was filed by the deadline. new deadline for filing in order to avoid another automatic continuance is noon on 7/15/20.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 2003 ANNA L. GERMANO REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Validity Of Trust, Confirm Successor Trustee, And Confirm That Merrill Lynch Account Number 7z4-23714 Is A Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/22/2020. Examiner notes emailed 07/02/2020.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA DIANE PRICE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. close by 10/6/2021.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (Telephonic appearance encouraged.)
